analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in the model is lowered to the cuBLAS/CUTLASS SIMT (CUDA-core) single-precision path because the model runs in fp32. The kernels are literally named 'cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_*' and report tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 and smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 across all four projection types. On a Blackwell datacenter part the tensor pipeline is idle while the SIMT FMA path does all the work, so the four GEMM families (together ~84% of attributed time) run far below available throughput. Achieved occupancy is also very low (8-20%) because the SIMT sgemm tiles carry 128-210 registers/thread, capping resident warps.

transformation.description: Promote the GEMM operands (and surrounding activation tensors) from fp32 to bf16 so Inductor lowers aten.mm/aten.addmm to the tensor-core HGEMM path (cutlass h884gemm / cublasLt bf16) instead of cutlass_80_simt_sgemm. bf16 is preferred over tf32 because Blackwell tensor cores deliver the largest speedup at bf16 and GPT-2 inference is numerically tolerant; if accuracy regresses, fall back to TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True) which keeps fp32 storage but still engages the tensor pipeline. Insert an aten._to_copy(dtype=bf16) on each fp32 mm/addmm input and an _to_copy back to fp32 where a downstream fp32 consumer requires it; keep LayerNorm reductions in fp32 for stability.
transformation.location: All 48 GEMM nodes across the 12 transformer blocks (QKV addmm, attn-out mm, FFN fc_up mm, FFN fc_down mm).
fx_steps: for node in gm.graph.find_nodes(op='call_function', target=torch.ops.aten.mm.default) + gm.graph.find_nodes(op='call_function', target=torch.ops.aten.addmm.default):
cast_inputs = [gm.graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten._to_copy.default, (arg,), {'dtype': torch.bfloat16}) if is_fp32_tensor(arg) else arg for arg in node.args]
node.replace_all_uses_with(back, delete_user_cb=lambda u: u is not back)
transformation.code_hint: Preferred: do the cast once at a module-boundary autocast(dtype=bf16) region so Inductor fuses the cast into the producing pointwise/LayerNorm epilogue and the redundant fp32<->bf16 round-trips collapse; per-node _to_copy is the explicit-FX fallback.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~2x on GEMM operand reads (bf16 halves weight + activation bytes); the larger benefit is engaging the tensor pipeline so the SIMT FMA bound is removed.

optimizations.notes: Highest-value change by far. duration_ns is ncu-inflated (2-5x) so the 52% figure is a within-profile relative estimate, not wall-clock. After this pass the redundant per-node fp32<->bf16 casts should be fused away by Inductor or removed by OPT-4. Must run before any buffer-registering pass per the buffer-dtype rule.

optimizations.match_target: F.scaled_dot_product_attention (formed from the fused QKV projection output)

bottleneck.description: The model already emits a single fused QKV projection per layer (out_features = 2304 = 3x768, the addmm with weight [768,2304]), so Q/K/V are NOT three separate small GEMMs -- that part is already optimal. The cost is in the hand-built attention path: a large fused Triton kernel (triton_poi_fused__scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention_cat_constant_pad_nd_expand_scalar_tensor_slice_split_transpose_view_where) that splits the 2304 output, transposes to [B,H,S,D], constant-pads, builds a causal mask via scalar_tensor/where, then calls _efficient_attention_forward. This prep family (aten::view_0) is 48 launches and fully memory-bound (L1 hit 0%, L2 hit 1%, sm_throughput 1.7%, dram 22%, occupancy 33%) -- pure layout massaging. The attention compute is fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80 (fp32 mem-efficient, NOT flash, no tensor cores).

transformation.description: Rewrite the attention region to a single F.scaled_dot_product_attention call with is_causal=True, letting Inductor select the flash/mem-efficient backend and (once OPT-1 makes Q/K/V bf16) route it to the tensor-core flash kernel. Matching at the functional level keys on the single post-LayerNorm activation feeding the fused QKV linear and the qkv-output node; the matcher splits the 2304 dim into q,k,v, reshapes to [B,H,S,D], and emits F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q,k,v,is_causal=True). This eliminates the explicit constant_pad_nd / scalar_tensor / where causal-mask construction (the mask becomes the is_causal flag) and the manual cat/transpose/split prep, collapsing the 48-launch memory-bound view_0 family.
transformation.location: Attention subgraph of each of the 12 blocks: between the QKV linear output and the attn-output linear input.
fx_steps: qkv_linears = find F.linear nodes whose weight second-dim == 3*hidden (2304)
locate the split/reshape chain producing q,k,v and the existing _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention / _efficient_attention_forward node
erase the now-dead constant_pad_nd, scalar_tensor, where, expand mask-construction nodes
transformation.code_hint: Use is_causal=True instead of materializing an additive mask so the where/scalar_tensor/constant_pad_nd nodes become dead. Verify F.scaled_dot_product_attention signature (query, key, value, attn_mask=None, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False, scale=None) before emitting; seq_len=128 is static so no dynamic-shape guard issue.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes 48 memory-bound layout/mask kernels (~75 MB of redundant DRAM reads on the view_0 family) and lets attention read fused QKV directly; with OPT-1 the fmha kernel becomes a bf16 tensor-core flash kernel.

optimizations.notes: QKV is already fused in this trace, so the value is in canonicalizing to F.scaled_dot_product_attention + is_causal to kill the 48-launch memory-bound prep family and enable the bf16 tensor-core attention backend. Confidence medium because the split/reshape graph must be walked carefully and mis-identifying the causal path would change numerics. Per the structural-constraint table SDPA replacement must follow QKV fusion -- QKV is pre-fused here, and OPT-1 is satisfied-by-level (functional precedes aten in the funnel).

bottleneck.description: This is inference (eval/no_grad) with constant weights, but the model is not frozen, so Inductor cannot pre-transpose/pre-pack the weight constants into the tensor-core-friendly layout or constant-fold the LayerNorm affine + projection bias into the GEMM epilogue. The GEMMs use 'nn' (non-transposed) CUTLASS variants and the bias/GELU/LN epilogues run as separate memory-bound Triton kernels (aten::addmm_0 family: 254689 ns, 72 launches, dram 42-50%, tensor_core 0%). High L2 hit (86-91%) on the GEMMs shows weights are resident, so the win is layout/epilogue, not caching.

transformation.description: Enable Inductor freezing (config.freezing = True) for this inference graph so constant weights are folded and pre-transposed/pre-packed into the tensor-core layout, and bias/LayerNorm-affine epilogues can be fused into the GEMM. Combined with OPT-1's bf16 promotion this lets cublasLt/CUTLASS pick a fused-epilogue HGEMM, removing several standalone memory-bound bias-add/GELU Triton kernels in the addmm_0 family. (channels_last is irrelevant for 2D activations; the relevant lowering decision is weight pre-transpose, which freezing owns.)
transformation.location: compile_fx config_patches for the whole module (applies to all 48 GEMMs + their epilogues).
fx_steps: # freezing constant-folds the LayerNorm affine + projection bias and pre-transposes weight constants into the tensor-core path
transformation.code_hint: freezing is only sound under inference/no_grad (true here). It must run AFTER bf16 promotion so the frozen/pre-packed weight constant is materialized at bf16 (buffer-dtype rule); being inductor_config it is satisfied-by-level after the aten-level OPT-1.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Fuses bias-add (triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_view_6, dram 43%) and part of the LN/GELU epilogue into the GEMM, removing standalone elementwise passes over the 512x768 / 512x3072 activations.

optimizations.notes: Pure config-level lever, low risk, but realized benefit depends on cublasLt/Inductor actually selecting a fused-epilogue HGEMM after OPT-1. Must come after OPT-1 so frozen weights are packed at bf16; no within-level prerequisite needed (different IR level).

optimizations.match_target: torch.ops.aten._to_copy.default (redundant fp32<->bf16 pairs introduced by OPT-1)
operators: aten::addmm_0 fused pointwise chain between consecutive GEMMs (72 nodes)

bottleneck.description: OPT-1 inserts an _to_copy(bf16) before every GEMM and an _to_copy(fp32) after it. Where two GEMMs are connected through a pointwise op (fc_up -> GELU -> fc_down, or QKV -> attention -> attn-out), this creates a fp32->bf16->...->fp32->bf16 round-trip whose only effect is extra DRAM traffic. The epilogue kernels (aten::addmm_0 family) are already memory-bound (dram 42-50%), so uncancelled casts would further inflate memory traffic over the same activations.

evidence.kernel: triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_mul_pow_tanh_view_5 (GELU, dram 50.16%) sits between fc_up and fc_down GEMMs

transformation.description: Walk the graph and cancel adjacent inverse _to_copy pairs (fp32->bf16->fp32 and bf16->fp32->bf16) introduced by OPT-1, then push remaining casts into the producing pointwise/LayerNorm epilogue so each transformer block runs bf16 end-to-end with a single cast at the embedding output and a single cast back before the final LayerNorm/logits. GEMMs stay on the tensor-core path while the redundant memory round-trips disappear.
transformation.location: All cast nodes inserted by OPT-1, especially the fc_up->GELU->fc_down and QKV->attn->attn-out chains in each of the 12 blocks.
fx_steps: if inp.op == 'call_function' and inp.target == torch.ops.aten._to_copy.default and node.kwargs.get('dtype') == orig_dtype_of(inp.args[0]):
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent to running an autocast(bf16) region over the whole block instead of per-node casts; then this pass is a no-op cleanup. Keep LayerNorm reductions in fp32.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes redundant fp32<->bf16 round-trips on the 512x768 and 512x3072 activations between consecutive GEMMs; cuts repeated full-tensor reads in the GELU/bias epilogues.

optimizations.notes: Cleanup that only has work to do if OPT-1 inserts per-node casts; if OPT-1 is a block-level autocast region this pass is unnecessary. Operates on OPT-1's cast nodes, so OPT-1 -> OPT-4 is a genuine within-level (aten) ordering constraint.

global_notes: Knowledge files knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repo; fallback applied. No device entry was available, so Blackwell-specific limits were inferred from the trace: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null throughout (consistent with the Blackwell counter removal), so latency-bound diagnosis used eligible_cycles_pct and achieved_occupancy. sm_count for RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell was not looked up, so Waves/SM was not computed numerically.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were not invoked; FX API patterns came from built-in knowledge. Per fallback policy, novel transforms are capped at 'medium' confidence -- hence OPT-2/OPT-3/OPT-4 are medium even where evidence is strong.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: many operators have is_fused=true (embedding, view_0/SDPA-prep, addmm_0/LN-GELU). The catch-all fused operators (aten::addmm_0, aten::view_0) aggregate Triton kernels carrying aten names from several ops; their time is kept as separate budget line items and not double-counted against the GEMM operators.

edge_case_flag high_unattributed: a large fraction of kernels land in unattributed_kernels[]. Two groups: (1) pre-NVTX warm-up/initialization kernels (explicitly 'flagged as initialization kernel'), correctly excluded; (2) runtime triton_per_fused_native_layer_norm_0 and triton_poi_fused__scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention_..._where_3 kernels with empty metrics raw:{} that received no ncu counters. The standalone LayerNorm and the SDPA-prep _3 variant are therefore under-counted in the budget; their true share folds conceptually into OPT-2 (SDPA prep) and a LayerNorm-fusion opportunity that could not be quantified.

Dynamic-shape check: GEMM durations are stable across the 12 layer instances (QKV ~59.9us, attn-out ~32.7us, fc_up ~84.6us, fc_down ~88-89us; coefficient of variation well under 50%), so the dynamic_shapes flag is NOT set and batch-padding was not disabled on variance grounds. seq_len=128 and batch=4 are static; the 512x768 problem (512 = 4*128) tiles cleanly, so batch-padding offers no benefit anyway.

DOMINANT BOTTLENECK: fp32 forces every GEMM onto the cutlass_80_simt_sgemm SIMT path with tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 across all 48 GEMM launches (~84% of attributed time). OPT-1 (bf16/tf32 dtype promotion) is the single highest-impact change and is the prerequisite-by-level for OPT-3 (freezing/weight pre-pack) and a direct prerequisite for OPT-4 (cast cleanup). Secondary bottleneck: the hand-built attention prep (aten::view_0, 48 memory-bound launches at <2% SM throughput) plus a non-tensor-core fp32 mem-efficient attention kernel, addressed by OPT-2.

Dependency DAG (no cycles): OPT-1 (aten, dtype) -> OPT-4 (aten, cast-cancel) is a within-level edge. OPT-2 (functional, SDPA) precedes OPT-1 by funnel level so bf16 attention operands are produced automatically. OPT-3 (inductor_config, freezing) runs last in the funnel and is satisfied-by-level after OPT-1 so frozen weights pack at bf16. Recommended application order: OPT-2, OPT-1, OPT-4, OPT-3.

All duration_ns values are ncu counter-collection inflated (2-5x) and used only for relative comparison within this profile, never as absolute latency. Time-budget percentages are shares of total_attributed_ns (~3.79M ns). Operators below 1% were omitted from further reasoning: aten::embedding (8160 ns, 0.22%) and aten::arange (1472 ns, 0.04%) -- 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.26% of attributed time).
